comments_critique.summary: The discussion centers on the technical performance and nostalgic appeal of a physics-based web demo. Users report issues with browser compatibility and physics solver stability, while also reminiscing about similar past experiences.

comments.author: jaen
comments.text: What's the physics solver used here? This demonstration quickly runs into common problems associated with overly simplistic solvers/integrators where everything starts to become unstable and "vibrate" / "twitch".
comments.analysis: This comment accurately identifies common issues in physics simulations related to solver stability, showing technical understanding and constructive critique.

comments.author: Tepix
comments.text: It seems to be broken on Firefox. It shows 3/8 players, the circles are flickering madly and I can't drag them.
comments.analysis: The user reports specific technical problems with the demo on Firefox, which is plausible and useful feedback, though it lacks deeper analysis.

comments.author: coldcity_again
comments.text:
Bit twitchy indeed but still fun. Instantly took me back to "rubber glenz vectors" in classic Amiga prods[1]

[1] https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OZTnR3FpUEA&start=413
comments.analysis: This comment provides a nostalgic and subjective perspective rather than technical insight, making it less informative about the demo's issues.
